ticket.description:
Hi Touseef

 

Please can you refer to the 2 emails below and advise?

 

Sorry to be a pain.

 

Kind regards

 

Jakki Waters

Business Operations Manager

 


       01482 711259

       Alexis Filters, Unit 20, Melton Enterprise Park, Redcliff Road, North Ferriby, HU14 3RS

       www.alexisfilters.co.uk (http://www.alexisfilters.co.uk/)

       Jakki.Waters@alexisfilters.co.uk

  

 

 

 

 

To view our privacy policy see Our Privacy Policy (https://www.alexisfilters.co.uk/privacy-policy)

If you are not the intended recipient you must not disclose or use the information for any purpose Please delete the email immediately. As part of conducting its business, Alexis Filters LTD may monitor and record emails and their contents passing through their network under the Telecommunications Regulations 2000. Although we have checked this e-mail for viruses, it is your responsibility to scan the message and attachments prior to opening them. We do not accept any responsibility for the consequences of passing on any virus.

 

 

From: Vinny Shone <vinny@clickconvertmarketing.com>
Sent: 24 April 2026 10:31
To: Jakki Waters <jakki.waters@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Cc: William Hansard <Billy.hansard@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Subject: Re: FW: [##31020##] Your ticket has been Closed

 

Hi Jakki,

It would be best to to loop in PluginHive support to resolve a VAT double-charging issue.

The Problem: > FedEx is sending their live API rates already inclusive of VAT. However, WooCommerce expects shipping rates to be exclusive of VAT so it can apply the taxes natively. As Billy noticed, this is causing WooCommerce to add VAT on top of an already-taxed FedEx rate at checkout.

What We Need to Know:
Can this be fixed within the PluginHive settings, or does FedEx need to change how rates are returned at the account level?

Acceptable Solutions:

Ideal: FedEx/PluginHive sends the raw, tax-exclusive base rate to WooCommerce.

Alternative: PluginHive separates the shipping charge from the VAT before passing it to WooCommerce.

We need WooCommerce to handle the tax calculation natively so that our VAT liabilities are recorded accurately in both WooCommerce and Sage.

 

On Fri, 24 Apr 2026 at 10:12, Jakki Waters <jakki.waters@alexisfilters.co.uk> wrote:

Hi Vinny

 

Before I go back to plus in Hive on this, I wanted to check that this isn’t a setting on our side that is duplicating the tax per the below email.

 

Can you please advise?

 

Kind regards

 

Jakki Waters

Business Operations Manager

 


       01482 711259

       Alexis Filters, Unit 20, Melton Enterprise Park, Redcliff Road, North Ferriby, HU14 3RS

       www.alexisfilters.co.uk (http://www.alexisfilters.co.uk/)

       Jakki.Waters@alexisfilters.co.uk

  

 

 

 

 

To view our privacy policy see Our Privacy Policy (https://www.alexisfilters.co.uk/privacy-policy)

From: William Hansard <Billy.hansard@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Sent: 24 April 2026 10:11
To: Jakki Waters <jakki.waters@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Subject: RE: [##31020##] Your ticket has been Closed

 

Hi Jakki,

 

I have enabled the tax option on WooCommerce,

It is now charging the customer tax on the shipping when one of the options is selected,


However, tax is already included in the FedEx price, and this has not been taken off the cost in the shipping option,

Please see below on how we are charged for shipping through FedEx.

 

 

With this new option enabled, we are charging the customer for tax twice.

 

Many thanks,

 

Billy

 

Billy Hansard

Customer Service & Digital Support

 


       01482 711259

       Alexis Filters, Unit 20, Melton Enterprise Park, Redcliff Road, North Ferriby, HU14 3RS

       www.alexisfilters.co.uk (http://www.alexisfilters.co.uk/)

       billy.hansard@alexisfilters.co.uk

From: Vinny Shone <vinny@clickconvertmarketing.com>
Sent: 24 April 2026 10:31
To: Jakki Waters <jakki.waters@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Cc: William Hansard <Billy.hansard@alexisfilters.co.uk>
Subject: Re: FW: [##31020##] Your ticket has been Closed

 

Hi Jakki,

It would be best to to loop in PluginHive support to resolve a VAT double-charging issue.

The Problem: > FedEx is sending their live API rates already inclusive of VAT. However, WooCommerce expects shipping rates to be exclusive of VAT so it can apply the taxes natively. As Billy noticed, this is causing WooCommerce to add VAT on top of an already-taxed FedEx rate at checkout.

What We Need to Know:
Can this be fixed within the PluginHive settings, or does FedEx need to change how rates are returned at the account level?

Acceptable Solutions:

Ideal: FedEx/PluginHive sends the raw, tax-exclusive base rate to WooCommerce.

Alternative: PluginHive separates the shipping charge from the VAT before passing it to WooCommerce.

We need WooCommerce to handle the tax calculation natively so that our VAT liabilities are recorded accurately in both WooCommerce and Sage.

 

On Fri, 24 Apr 2026 at 10:12, Jakki Waters <jakki.waters@alexisfilters.co.uk> wrote:

Hi Vinny

 

Before I go back to plus in Hive on this, I wanted to check that this isn’t a setting on our side that is duplicating the tax per the below email.

 

Can you please advise?

 

Kind regards

 

Jakki Waters

Business Operations Manager

 


       01482 711259

Subject: RE: [##31020##] Your ticket has been Closed

 

Hi Jakki,

 

I have enabled the tax option on WooCommerce,

It is now charging the customer tax on the shipping when one of the options is selected,


However, tax is already included in the FedEx price, and this has not been taken off the cost in the shipping option,

Please see below on how we are charged for shipping through FedEx.

 

 

With this new option enabled, we are charging the customer for tax twice.

 

Many thanks,

 

Billy

Thank you for your patience.

We will be enhancing the plugin to return the FedEx rates on checkout without the Vat and taxes.

This will be made available in the upcoming release of the plugin which is tentatively scheduled for third week of May.

We will update you as soon as the enhancement is made available.

Feel free to reach out if you have any further queries in the meantime.

Thanks and Regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
